name: Signet Credential Engine
description: Self-issuance credential engine. Takes structured data from vault and produces credentials in two formats: (1) SD-JWT VC per RFC 9901 using sd-jwt-rust (OpenWallet Foundation) for baseline interoperability, and (2) BBS+ signed attribute sets using anoncreds-v2-rs for unlinkable presentations. Pre-computes boolean attributes (age_over_21, balance_above_1000, etc.) in BBS+ credentials to avoid ZK circuits. Generates Pedersen commitments for numeric attributes (for downstream Bulletproof range proofs). TTL management with expiry tracking. One-time credential consumption tracking. Credentials signed by vault's identity key. Credential storage and caching within vault. Signing is delegated to signet_vault via Signer trait — the cred engine never touches raw key material. All algorithms are compile-time fixed (no negotiation). CBOR is the primary internal serialization format. Oracle-safe error types leak no secrets.

types.name: CredentialId

fields.name: value
fields.description: Hex-encoded 128-bit unique identifier

validators.error_message: CredentialId must be exactly 32 hex characters

types.description: Globally unique credential identifier. 128-bit random, encoded as 32-char hex string.

types.name: Domain

fields.name: value
fields.description: DNS-style domain identifier (e.g., 'bank.example.com')

validators.error_message: Domain must be valid DNS-style identifier

types.description: Domain binding for credential scope. Represents the relying party or service domain a credential is bound to.

types.name: ClaimPath

fields.name: pointer
fields.description: RFC 6901 JSON Pointer string, e.g., '/personal/date_of_birth'

validators.error_message: ClaimPath must be a valid non-empty JSON Pointer

types.description: JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) path to a claim within a ClaimSet. Used to reference source data for derived attributes.

types.name: ClaimValue
types.description: Typed claim value for use in predicates and raw attributes. Restricted to types that have well-defined BBS+ scalar encodings.

types.name: Predicate
types.description: Closed set of predicates for pre-computing DerivedBoolean attributes. No custom predicates allowed — this is a fixed enum to avoid ZK circuit complexity.

types.name: AttributeKind
types.description: Three-category attribute model. Raw for direct disclosure, DerivedBoolean for pre-computed predicate results (avoids ZK circuits), Committed for numeric values hidden behind Pedersen commitments (enabling downstream Bulletproof range proofs).

types.name: RawAttribute

fields.name: name
fields.description: Attribute name matching schema field name

fields.name: value
fields.description: The claim value to be included directly

types.description: A directly-disclosable claim value.

types.name: DerivedBooleanAttribute

fields.name: name
fields.description: Attribute name (e.g., 'age_over_21')

fields.name: predicate
fields.description: The predicate evaluated against the source claim

fields.name: source_path
fields.description: JSON Pointer to the source claim in the ClaimSet

fields.name: value
fields.description: Pre-computed result of evaluating the predicate

types.description: A boolean attribute pre-computed from a predicate over a source claim. Example: age_over_21 = GreaterThanOrEqual(21) applied to /personal/age.

types.name: PedersenCommitment

fields.name: commitment_bytes
fields.description: Compressed Ristretto point (32 bytes)

validators.error_message: Pedersen commitment must be exactly 32 bytes (compressed Ristretto)

fields.name: generator_domain_tag
fields.description: Domain separation tag used for generator derivation, always 'signet-cred-pedersen-v1'

types.description: A Pedersen commitment to a numeric value. Generator points derived via hash-to-curve with domain separation tag 'signet-cred-pedersen-v1'. The commitment is C = v*G + r*H where v is the value and r is the blinding factor.

types.name: CommittedAttribute

fields.name: name
fields.description: Attribute name (e.g., 'account_balance')

fields.name: commitment
fields.description: The Pedersen commitment to the numeric value

fields.name: source_path
fields.description: JSON Pointer to the source numeric claim

types.description: A numeric attribute hidden behind a Pedersen commitment for downstream Bulletproof range proofs.

types.name: AttributeEntry

fields.name: index
fields.description: Deterministic position in the BBS+ message vector. Assigned by CredentialSchema field ordering.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: Which category this attribute belongs to

fields.name: raw
fields.description: Present when kind == Raw

fields.name: derived_boolean
fields.description: Present when kind == DerivedBoolean

fields.name: committed
fields.description: Present when kind == Committed

types.description: A single entry in the credential attribute vector. Wraps all three attribute kinds with a deterministic ordering index for BBS+ message vector mapping.

types.name: SchemaField

fields.name: name
fields.description: Field name in the output credential

fields.name: kind
fields.description: Which attribute category this field uses

fields.name: source_path
fields.description: JSON Pointer to the source claim in the input ClaimSet

fields.name: predicate
fields.description: Required when kind == DerivedBoolean. The predicate to evaluate.

fields.name: required
fields.description: Whether this field must be present in the ClaimSet

types.description: A single field definition within a CredentialSchema. Defines the attribute kind, source path, and optional predicate for derived booleans.

types.name: DisclosureLevel
types.description: Three-level disclosure policy for selective disclosure in SD-JWT and BBS+ presentations.

types.name: DisclosureRule

fields.name: field_name
fields.description: Must match a SchemaField.name in the associated schema

fields.name: level
fields.description: Disclosure policy for this field

types.description: Maps a schema field name to its disclosure level.

types.name: DisclosurePolicy

fields.name: rules
fields.description: Per-field disclosure rules

fields.name: default_level
fields.description: Default disclosure level for fields not explicitly listed. Defaults to Never (fail-closed).

types.description: Governs selective disclosure across all fields in a credential. Every schema field must have a rule; missing rules default to Never (fail-closed).

types.name: CredentialSchema

fields.name: schema_id
fields.description: Unique schema identifier

fields.name: version
fields.description: Schema version number, monotonically increasing

fields.name: fields
fields.description: Ordered list of field definitions. Order determines BBS+ message vector indices.

validators.error_message: Schema must have at least one field

fields.name: disclosure_policy
fields.description: Selective disclosure policy for this schema

fields.name: description
fields.description: Human-readable description of the credential type

types.description: Defines the shape, attribute types, field ordering, and disclosure policy for a credential. Field ordering is deterministic and defines the BBS+ message vector index mapping.

types.name: ClaimSet

fields.name: claims
fields.description: Map from JSON Pointer path strings to ClaimValue entries

fields.name: source_vault_id
fields.description: Identifier of the vault compartment this data was retrieved from

fields.name: retrieved_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the data was retrieved from vault

types.description: Structured input data from the vault for credential issuance. Key-value map where keys are JSON Pointer paths and values are typed claim values.

types.name: CredentialMetadata

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique credential identifier

fields.name: schema_id
fields.description: Schema identifier this credential conforms to

fields.name: schema_version
fields.description: Schema version at time of issuance

fields.name: issued_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of issuance

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of expiry

fields.name: domain
fields.description: Domain this credential is bound to

fields.name: one_time
fields.description: If true, credential transitions to Consumed after first presentation

fields.name: issuer_public_key_id
fields.description: Identifier of the vault signing key used to issue this credential

types.description: Metadata associated with an issued credential. Tracks identity, timing, domain binding, and one-time usage flag.

types.name: SdJwtCredential

fields.name: compact
fields.description: SD-JWT compact serialization string (header.payload.signature~disclosure1~disclosure2~...)

fields.name: disclosures
fields.description: Individual base64url-encoded disclosure strings

fields.name: key_binding_required
fields.description: Whether key binding JWT is required for presentation

types.description: SD-JWT VC per RFC 9901 in compact serialization. The JWT is signed with Ed25519 via vault's identity key.

types.name: BbsMessage

fields.name: index
fields.description: Position in the message vector

fields.name: scalar_bytes
fields.description: Canonical scalar encoding of the attribute value (32 bytes)

validators.error_message: BBS+ scalar must be exactly 32 bytes

types.description: A single message in the BBS+ message vector. Contains the scalar encoding of an attribute.

types.name: BbsSignature

fields.name: signature_bytes
fields.description: Serialized BBS+ signature

fields.name: public_key_bytes
fields.description: BBS+ public key used for verification

types.description: A BBS+ signature over the message vector.

types.name: BbsCredential

fields.name: signature
fields.description: BBS+ signature over the message vector

fields.name: messages
fields.description: Ordered message vector matching schema field ordering

fields.name: attributes
fields.description: Typed attribute entries corresponding to each message

fields.name: message_count
fields.description: Total number of messages in the vector

validators.error_message: Must have at least one message

types.description: BBS+ signed credential containing the signature and public message vector (including Pedersen commitments). Does NOT contain blinding factors or raw numeric values — those are in PrivateWitness.

types.name: BlindingFactor

fields.name: attribute_name
fields.description: Name of the committed attribute this blinding factor corresponds to

fields.name: factor_bytes
fields.description: 32-byte scalar blinding factor (Zeroize on drop)

validators.error_message: Blinding factor must be exactly 32 bytes

types.description: Blinding factor for a single Pedersen commitment. Must be zeroized on drop. Contains the random scalar r in C = v*G + r*H.

types.name: WitnessEntry

fields.name: attribute_name
fields.description: Name of the committed attribute

fields.name: raw_value
fields.description: The original numeric value (Zeroize on drop)

fields.name: blinding_factor
fields.description: Blinding factor for the Pedersen commitment

types.description: A single private witness entry pairing a raw numeric value with its blinding factor for a committed attribute.

types.name: PrivateWitness

fields.name: credential_id
fields.description: Credential this witness belongs to

fields.name: entries
fields.description: Private witness entries for each committed attribute

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of witness creation

types.description: Private material needed for downstream Bulletproof range proofs. Contains blinding factors and raw numeric values for all committed attributes. Implements ZeroizeOnDrop. Stored encrypted in vault, separate from public credential material.

types.name: CredentialBundle

fields.name: sd_jwt
fields.description: SD-JWT VC format credential for baseline interoperability

fields.name: bbs
fields.description: BBS+ signed credential for unlinkable presentations

fields.name: witness
fields.description: Private witness material (blinding factors, raw values). ZeroizeOnDrop.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Credential lifecycle metadata

types.description: Complete output of credential issuance. Contains both credential formats, private witness material, and metadata. The PrivateWitness is stored separately in the vault from the public credential data.

types.name: CredentialStatus
types.description: Credential lifecycle state machine. Transitions: Active -> Presented, Active -> Consumed (one-time only, atomic), Active -> Expired (TTL), Active -> Revoked, Presented -> Consumed (one-time only), Presented -> Expired, Presented -> Revoked. Terminal states: Consumed, Expired, Revoked.

types.name: PresentationRecord

fields.name: presented_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of presentation

fields.name: presented_to
fields.description: Domain the credential was presented to

fields.name: disclosed_fields
fields.description: List of field names that were disclosed in this presentation

types.description: Record of a credential presentation event.

types.name: CredentialRecord

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Credential metadata

fields.name: status
fields.description: Current lifecycle status

fields.name: presentation_history
fields.description: Ordered list of presentation events

fields.name: sd_jwt
fields.description: SD-JWT VC format credential

fields.name: bbs
fields.description: BBS+ format credential

types.description: Full credential record as stored in the vault. Combines metadata, status, and presentation history.

types.name: IssuanceConfig

fields.name: ttl_seconds
fields.description: Time-to-live in seconds from issuance

validators.error_message: TTL must be between 1 second and 1 year (31536000 seconds)

fields.name: domain
fields.description: Domain binding for the credential

fields.name: one_time
fields.description: If true, credential can only be presented once before transitioning to Consumed

fields.name: clock_skew_tolerance_seconds
fields.description: Allowed clock skew tolerance in seconds for expiry checks

validators.error_message: Clock skew tolerance must be 0-300 seconds

types.description: Configuration parameters for credential issuance.

types.name: CredentialEngineConfig

fields.name: default_ttl_seconds
fields.description: Default TTL for credentials if not specified in IssuanceConfig

validators.error_message: Default TTL must be between 1 second and 1 year

fields.name: default_clock_skew_tolerance_seconds
fields.description: Default clock skew tolerance

validators.error_message: Clock skew tolerance must be 0-300 seconds

fields.name: pedersen_domain_tag
fields.description: Domain separation tag for Pedersen generator derivation via hash-to-curve. Fixed at compile time.

fields.name: max_attributes_per_credential
fields.description: Maximum number of attributes in a single credential

types.description: Configuration for the CredentialEngine instance.

types.name: CredError
types.description: Oracle-safe error type for the credential engine. All Display implementations are constant-time-safe and never leak secret material. Crypto failures return generic error types to prevent oracle attacks.

types.name: CredErrorDetail

fields.name: kind
fields.description: Error category

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message. Must not contain secret material.

fields.name: credential_id
fields.description: Credential ID if applicable (safe to include as it is a public identifier)

types.description: Structured error with a CredError variant and a safe (non-secret-leaking) message. The message must not contain key material, blinding factors, or raw claim values.

types.name: AuditEventKind
types.description: Types of credential lifecycle events recorded to the audit log.

types.name: CredentialAuditEvent

fields.name: event_kind
fields.description: Type of event

fields.name: credential_id
fields.description: Credential this event pertains to

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the event

fields.name: domain
fields.description: Domain involved if applicable

fields.name: details
fields.description: Additional safe (non-secret) details

types.description: Audit event for credential lifecycle transitions. Recorded to vault's audit log on every state change.

types.name: ExpiryCheckResult

fields.name: credential_id
fields.description: Credential checked

fields.name: is_expired
fields.description: Whether the credential has expired

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 expiry timestamp

fields.name: remaining_seconds
fields.description: Seconds remaining until expiry (0 if expired)

fields.name: status_updated
fields.description: Whether the credential status was transitioned to Expired

types.description: Result of checking a credential's expiry status.

types.name: ScalarEncoding
types.description: Canonical encoding scheme for mapping ClaimValues to BBS+ scalars. Fixed at compile time — no negotiation.

functions.name: create_engine
functions.description: Construct a new CredentialEngine instance with the given configuration and vault signer. The engine holds a reference to the vault signer trait object and vault storage trait object. No raw key material is ever held by the engine.

inputs.name: signer
inputs.description: Reference to a signet_vault::Signer trait object for delegated signing

inputs.name: storage
inputs.description: Reference to a signet_vault::VaultStorage trait object for credential persistence

inputs.name: audit_log
inputs.description: Reference to a signet_vault::AuditLog trait object for event recording

error_cases.name: invalid_config
error_cases.condition: Config validation fails (e.g., TTL out of range)

error_cases.name: signer_unavailable
error_cases.condition: Signer trait object cannot retrieve public keys

preconditions: signer must be a valid Signer trait object from signet_vault
storage must be a valid VaultStorage trait object from signet_vault
audit_log must be a valid AuditLog trait object from signet_vault
config must pass all field validators
postconditions: Returned engine holds no raw key material
Engine is ready to issue credentials via issue_credential

functions.name: register_schema
functions.description: Register a CredentialSchema with the engine. Validates schema consistency: all DerivedBoolean fields must have predicates, disclosure policy must cover all fields, field names must be unique, and BBS+ message vector ordering is deterministic from field order. Schema registration is idempotent for the same schema_id + version.

error_cases.name: schema_violation_missing_predicate
error_cases.condition: A DerivedBoolean field has no predicate

error_cases.name: schema_violation_duplicate_field
error_cases.condition: Two or more fields have the same name

error_cases.name: invalid_disclosure_policy
error_cases.condition: Disclosure policy references a field name not in the schema

error_cases.name: attribute_limit_exceeded
error_cases.condition: Schema has more fields than max_attributes_per_credential

error_cases.name: vault_error_audit
error_cases.condition: Audit log recording fails

preconditions: Engine has been created via create_engine
Schema fields list is non-empty
All DerivedBoolean fields have a predicate defined
All field names within the schema are unique
Disclosure policy rules reference valid field names
postconditions: Schema is stored in the engine and available for issuance
BBS+ message vector index mapping is computed and cached
Audit event SchemaRegistered is recorded
Returns the schema_id on success

functions.name: issue_credential
functions.description: Issue a dual-format credential (SD-JWT VC + BBS+) from a ClaimSet and schema. Evaluates all predicates to pre-compute DerivedBoolean attributes. Generates Pedersen commitments for Committed attributes. Signs SD-JWT with Ed25519 via vault signer. Signs BBS+ message vector via vault signer. Generates PrivateWitness with blinding factors. Stores credential and witness separately in vault. Records audit event. Returns a complete CredentialBundle.

inputs.name: schema_id
inputs.description: ID of a previously registered schema

error_cases.name: schema_not_found
error_cases.condition: No schema with the given schema_id is registered

error_cases.name: missing_required_claim
error_cases.condition: A required schema field's source_path is not present in the ClaimSet

error_cases.name: claim_type_mismatch
error_cases.condition: A claim value's type does not match what the schema field expects (e.g., string for a Committed numeric field)

error_cases.name: invalid_predicate_evaluation
error_cases.condition: A predicate cannot be evaluated against the source claim (e.g., GreaterThan on a string)

error_cases.name: signing_failed_ed25519
error_cases.condition: Vault signer fails to produce Ed25519 signature (generic — no details exposed)

error_cases.name: signing_failed_bbs
error_cases.condition: Vault signer fails to produce BBS+ signature (generic — no details exposed)

error_cases.name: encoding_failed_sdjwt
error_cases.condition: SD-JWT encoding or serialization fails

error_cases.name: encoding_failed_cbor
error_cases.condition: CBOR serialization of BBS+ credential fails

error_cases.name: vault_storage_error
error_cases.condition: Vault storage fails to persist credential or witness

error_cases.name: vault_audit_error
error_cases.condition: Audit log recording fails

preconditions: Schema with schema_id has been registered via register_schema
All required schema fields are present in claims at their source_paths
ClaimSet values match expected types for their schema fields
Vault signer is available and can sign with both Ed25519 and BBS+
Vault storage is available for persistence
postconditions: CredentialBundle contains a valid SD-JWT VC with Ed25519 signature
CredentialBundle contains a valid BBS+ credential with correct message vector ordering
All DerivedBoolean attributes have been pre-computed from predicates
All Committed attributes have Pedersen commitments with fresh random blinding factors
PrivateWitness contains blinding factors and raw values for all committed attributes
Credential is stored in vault with status Active
PrivateWitness is stored encrypted in vault, separate from public credential
CredentialMetadata.id is a fresh random CredentialId
CredentialMetadata.expires_at = issued_at + config.ttl_seconds
Audit event CredentialIssued is recorded
All intermediate secret material (blinding factor generation state) is zeroized

structured_side_effects.description: Stores credential public material (CBOR-encoded) and private witness separately
No network calls — all operations are local

functions.name: get_credential
functions.description: Retrieve a credential record by ID from vault storage. Checks expiry and updates status to Expired if TTL has elapsed (accounting for clock skew tolerance). Does NOT return PrivateWitness — use get_witness separately.

inputs.name: credential_id
inputs.description: ID of the credential to retrieve

error_cases.name: credential_not_found
error_cases.condition: No credential with the given ID exists in vault storage

error_cases.name: decoding_failed
error_cases.condition: Stored credential data cannot be deserialized

error_cases.name: vault_error
error_cases.condition: Vault storage read fails

preconditions: Engine has been created via create_engine
Credential with the given ID exists in vault storage
postconditions: If credential TTL has elapsed, status is transitioned to Expired and audit event recorded
Returned CredentialRecord reflects current status
No private witness material is included in the response

structured_side_effects.description: May update status to Expired if TTL elapsed
Records CredentialExpired event if status transitioned

functions.name: get_witness
functions.description: Retrieve the PrivateWitness for a credential from vault storage. The returned witness contains blinding factors and raw numeric values needed for downstream Bulletproof range proofs. Caller is responsible for zeroizing the witness when done. Records audit event for witness access.

inputs.name: credential_id
inputs.description: ID of the credential whose witness to retrieve

error_cases.name: credential_not_found
error_cases.condition: No credential with the given ID exists

error_cases.name: witness_not_found
error_cases.condition: Credential exists but witness material has been deleted or was never stored

error_cases.name: credential_consumed
error_cases.condition: Credential is in Consumed state — witness should not be accessed

error_cases.name: credential_revoked
error_cases.condition: Credential is in Revoked state — witness should not be accessed

error_cases.name: credential_expired
error_cases.condition: Credential is in Expired state

error_cases.name: vault_error
error_cases.condition: Vault storage read fails

preconditions: Engine has been created via create_engine
Credential with the given ID exists and has associated witness material
Credential is not in a terminal state (Consumed, Revoked)
postconditions: Returned PrivateWitness contains all blinding factors and raw values for committed attributes
Audit event WitnessAccessed is recorded
Witness material in the return value implements ZeroizeOnDrop

functions.name: record_presentation
functions.description: Record that a credential was presented to a domain. Updates credential status from Active to Presented. For one-time credentials, atomically transitions to Consumed via vault's atomic_consume_credential. Records audit event. Validates that disclosed fields comply with the credential's DisclosurePolicy.

inputs.name: credential_id
inputs.description: ID of the credential being presented

inputs.name: presented_to
inputs.description: Domain the credential is being presented to

inputs.name: disclosed_fields
inputs.description: List of field name strings that were disclosed in this presentation

error_cases.name: credential_not_found
error_cases.condition: No credential with the given ID exists

error_cases.name: credential_expired
error_cases.condition: Credential TTL has elapsed

error_cases.name: credential_consumed
error_cases.condition: One-time credential has already been consumed

error_cases.name: credential_revoked
error_cases.condition: Credential has been revoked

error_cases.name: disclosure_policy_violation
error_cases.condition: Disclosed fields violate the DisclosurePolicy (e.g., a Never field was disclosed, or an Always field was omitted)

error_cases.name: status_transition_denied
error_cases.condition: Credential is in a terminal state that does not allow presentation

error_cases.name: atomic_consume_failed
error_cases.condition: Vault's atomic compare-and-swap for one-time consumption failed (race condition)

error_cases.name: vault_error
error_cases.condition: Vault storage update fails

preconditions: Credential exists and is in Active or Presented state
Credential is not expired (TTL check performed)
disclosed_fields only contains field names present in the credential's schema
disclosed_fields complies with DisclosurePolicy (no Never fields disclosed, all Always fields disclosed)
postconditions: If one_time credential and status was Active: status atomically transitions to Consumed
If one_time credential and status was Presented: status atomically transitions to Consumed
If not one_time: status transitions to Presented
PresentationRecord appended to credential's presentation_history
Audit event CredentialPresented (or CredentialConsumed for one-time) is recorded
For one-time credentials, witness material is marked for deletion
Returns the new CredentialStatus

structured_side_effects.description: Updates credential status and appends presentation record

functions.name: revoke_credential
functions.description: Revoke a credential, transitioning it to the terminal Revoked state. Deletes associated PrivateWitness from vault. Records audit event.

inputs.name: credential_id
inputs.description: ID of the credential to revoke

inputs.name: reason
inputs.description: Human-readable revocation reason (must not contain secret material)

error_cases.name: credential_not_found
error_cases.condition: No credential with the given ID exists

error_cases.name: already_consumed
error_cases.condition: Credential is already in Consumed terminal state

error_cases.name: already_revoked
error_cases.condition: Credential is already in Revoked terminal state

error_cases.name: vault_error
error_cases.condition: Vault storage update or witness deletion fails

preconditions: Credential exists and is not already in a terminal state (Consumed or Revoked)
postconditions: Credential status is Revoked
PrivateWitness material is deleted from vault and zeroized
Audit events CredentialRevoked and WitnessDeleted are recorded
Returns CredentialStatus::Revoked

structured_side_effects.description: Updates credential status to Revoked and deletes witness

functions.name: check_expiry
functions.description: Check whether a credential has expired based on its TTL and the configured clock skew tolerance. If expired, transitions status to Expired and records audit event. Returns detailed expiry information.

inputs.name: credential_id
inputs.description: ID of the credential to check

error_cases.name: credential_not_found
error_cases.condition: No credential with the given ID exists

error_cases.name: vault_error
error_cases.condition: Vault storage read or update fails

preconditions: Credential exists in vault storage
now is a valid ISO 8601 timestamp
postconditions: If credential has expired (expires_at + clock_skew_tolerance < now), status transitions to Expired
If status transitioned, audit event CredentialExpired is recorded
ExpiryCheckResult.status_updated is true only if a state transition occurred
ExpiryCheckResult.remaining_seconds is 0 if expired

functions.name: delete_witness
functions.description: Explicitly delete a PrivateWitness from vault storage. Used after Bulletproof proofs have been generated and the witness is no longer needed. Ensures zeroization of all blinding factors and raw values. Records audit event.

inputs.name: credential_id
inputs.description: ID of the credential whose witness to delete

error_cases.name: credential_not_found
error_cases.condition: No credential with the given ID exists

error_cases.name: witness_not_found
error_cases.condition: Witness material does not exist or was already deleted

error_cases.name: vault_error
error_cases.condition: Vault storage deletion fails

preconditions: Credential exists in vault storage
Witness material exists for this credential
postconditions: PrivateWitness material is deleted from vault storage
All blinding factors and raw values in the witness are zeroized
Audit event WitnessDeleted is recorded
Returns true on successful deletion

functions.name: encode_claim_to_scalar
functions.description: Encode a ClaimValue to a BBS+ scalar using the canonical encoding scheme. This is a pure function with no side effects. The encoding is deterministic: the same input always produces the same 32-byte scalar. Used internally during issuance and exposed for testing/verification.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: The encoding scheme to use (determined by value type)

error_cases.name: encoding_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Encoding scheme is incompatible with the value type

error_cases.name: value_out_of_range
error_cases.condition: Value cannot be represented as a valid scalar (e.g., integer too large)

preconditions: Encoding scheme is compatible with the value type (e.g., Int64LittleEndian for IntVal)
postconditions: Output is exactly 32 bytes
Encoding is deterministic (same input -> same output)
Output is a valid BBS+ scalar (less than the group order)

functions.name: validate_schema
functions.description: Validate a CredentialSchema without registering it. Checks all structural invariants: unique field names, DerivedBoolean fields have predicates, disclosure policy consistency, attribute count limits. Returns a list of validation error messages (empty list means valid).
postconditions: Returns empty list if schema is valid
Returns non-empty list of human-readable error strings if schema has issues
Does not modify any state

functions.name: evaluate_predicate
functions.description: Evaluate a Predicate against a ClaimValue to produce a boolean result. Pure function used during issuance to pre-compute DerivedBoolean attributes. Exposed for testing and verification.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The claim value to evaluate against

error_cases.name: type_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Value type is incompatible with the predicate (e.g., GreaterThan applied to a StringVal)

error_cases.name: empty_set
error_cases.condition: InSet predicate has an empty set

preconditions: Value type is compatible with the predicate (e.g., IntVal for GreaterThan)
postconditions: Returns true if the value satisfies the predicate, false otherwise
Evaluation is deterministic

signet_vault: VaultStorage::store_credential(id: CredentialId, public_cbor: bytes, private_cbor: bytes) -> Result<None, VaultError>

invariants: The credential engine NEVER holds or accesses raw key material — all signing is delegated to vault via Signer trait
All algorithms are compile-time fixed: Ed25519 for SD-JWT, BBS+ for unlinkable credentials, Pedersen over Ristretto for commitments. No algorithm negotiation in any code path.
PrivateWitness implements ZeroizeOnDrop — all blinding factors and raw numeric values are zeroized when the witness goes out of scope
All intermediate secret material (blinding factor generation state, scalar intermediates) is zeroized immediately after use
Error messages NEVER contain key material, blinding factors, raw claim values, or any secret data (oracle-safe)
Crypto failures (signing, commitment generation) return generic error types — no information leakage about failure mode
One-time credential consumption (Active/Presented -> Consumed) is atomic via vault's compare-and-swap — no race conditions
CredentialStatus state machine transitions are strictly enforced: Consumed, Expired, and Revoked are terminal states with no outbound transitions
BBS+ message vector ordering is deterministic: defined by CredentialSchema field list order, immutable once schema is registered

Canonical ClaimValue-to-scalar encoding is deterministic and fixed: Utf8HashToScalar for strings, Int64LittleEndian for integers, BoolSingleByte for booleans, BytesDirect for bytes, DateToEpochDays for dates
Pedersen commitment generators are derived via hash-to-curve with domain separation tag 'signet-cred-pedersen-v1' — generator derivation is deterministic and verifiable
SD-JWT VC output conforms to RFC 9901 compact serialization
All credential state transitions generate an audit event on the vault's audit log — no silent state changes
DisclosurePolicy defaults to Never (fail-closed) for any field not explicitly listed in rules
Pipeline timeout = deny: any vault operation timeout results in an error return, never a silent success
One-way data flow: credential data flows vault -> cred engine -> caller, never reverse. The cred engine does not write back to claim source.
BBS+ credentials must maintain unlinkability: no credential-specific identifiers are included in the BBS+ message vector that could correlate presentations
Clock skew tolerance is bounded (0-300 seconds) and applied consistently to all expiry checks
CBOR is the primary internal serialization format for vault storage; SD-JWT uses compact string serialization for external interoperability
All public types use serde(deny_unknown_fields) to reject unexpected data during deserialization
requires: signet_vault must expose a Signer trait with sign_ed25519 and sign_bbs methods
signet_vault must expose a VaultStorage trait for credential persistence
signet_vault must support atomic compare-and-swap for one-time credential consumption
signet_vault must expose an AuditLog trait for recording credential lifecycle events
signet_vault must zeroize all secret material on drop
sd-jwt-rust (OpenWallet Foundation) crate available for SD-JWT VC encoding
anoncreds-v2-rs crate available for BBS+ signature operations
dalek bulletproofs crate available for Pedersen commitment generation
System clock is reasonably accurate (clock skew tolerance is configurable)
